headline: The White House rethinks its Anthropic fight
subtitle: Government reverses Anthropic stance · Gemini arrives in Google cars · Stress-testing ideas with Perplexity
intro: The White House is shifting its confrontational stance toward Anthropic, driven partly by the company's powerful Mythos AI capabilities and national security concerns. A new administration memo aims to walk back the Pentagon's hard line while securing better government access to the frontier model.

bullets.text: White House limits Anthropic's private sector access expansion citing compute constraints
bullets.summary: The White House pushed back on Anthropic's plan to expand private sector access to Mythos from roughly 50 firms to 120, citing concerns about compute strains affecting government use. A national security memo will reportedly allow agencies to work around supply chain risk designations despite ongoing legal disputes.

bullets.text: Google rolls out Gemini for vehicles, replacing Assistant with conversational AI
bullets.summary: Google is upgrading its in-car AI system with Gemini, adding hands-free controls, navigation customization, and vehicle-specific assistance. The feature will roll out first to U.S. vehicles with Google built-in, including 4 million compatible General Motors vehicles from 2022 onward.

bullets.text: Using Perplexity Deep Research to systematically evaluate business ideas
bullets.summary: The newsletter shared a workflow for leveraging Perplexity's Deep Research mode to stress test startup concepts. Users can run the same prompt weekly on new ideas and generate validation slides in minutes, potentially accelerating idea evaluation significantly.

bullets.text: OpenAI traces ChatGPT's goblin obsession to single personality reward signal
bullets.summary: OpenAI discovered that its 'Nerdy' personality preset drove two-thirds of goblin mentions in user conversations, with the preference bleeding into other modes through fine-tuning loops. The company retired the personality and added explicit creature-blocking to GPT-5.5.

main_story.title: White House recalibrates Anthropic strategy amid national security tensions
main_story.body: After months of escalating conflict between the Pentagon and Anthropic, the White House is now attempting a delicate repositioning. The government wants better access to Anthropic's powerful Mythos model for national security purposes while simultaneously preventing the company from expanding private sector access due to compute constraints. A forthcoming national security memo will reportedly help agencies work around existing supply chain designations, effectively softening the administration's public hard line. This reversal appears driven partly by recognition that Mythos and competing frontier models like GPT 5.5 have reached cyber capabilities that make the government-vs-company conflict seem less strategically sound.
main_story.why: The Mythos breakthrough in AI cyber capabilities has fundamentally altered the White House's calculus around Anthropic, transforming what seemed like a straightforward ideological or corporate disagreement into a complex national security optimization problem. Policymakers now face pressure to balance access needs against concerns about compute availability.
main_story.take: The administration's pivot reveals that national security interests ultimately trump ideological opposition or corporate conflicts, though internal divisions remain visible with Pentagon officials still publicly critical of Anthropic's leadership.
